title: Orthodox stance

infobox.value: is one in which the fighter places their left foot in front, thus placing their left side closer to the opponent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Because it places the right side (the stronger side for most people) in the rear, the orthodox stance can allow for more rotation and distance to accelerate right-sided strikes, in turn generating more power.[[2]](#cite_note-2) This makes it the most common stance in boxing,[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) as well as MMA,[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) and primarily used by right-handed fighters.

["Which Is the Best Fighting Stance: Southpaw or Orthodox?"](https://legendsonlyleague.com/southpaw-vs-orthodox/). 21 June 2022. Retrieved 26 October 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Way of positioning the feet and hands in combat sports

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Some of this article's [listed sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") **may not be [reliable](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources")**. Please help improve this article by looking for better, more reliable sources. Unreliable citations may be challenged and removed. *(February 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Adolf_Grohs_Boxer_Kurt_Prenzel_Bildseite_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Adolf_Grohs_Boxer_Kurt_Prenzel_Bildseite_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Adolf_Grohs_Boxer_Kurt_Prenzel_Bildseite_(cropped).jpg)

Kurt Prenzel, boxer of the 1920s, displaying orthodox stance with left hand and left foot to the fore

In [combat sports](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport") such as [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") and [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") ("MMA"), an **orthodox stance** is one in which the fighter places their left foot in front, thus placing their left side closer to the opponent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Because it places the right side (the stronger side for most people) in the rear, the orthodox stance can allow for more rotation and distance to accelerate right-sided strikes, in turn generating more power.[[2]](#cite_note-2) This makes it the most common stance in boxing,[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) as well as MMA,[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) and primarily used by right-handed fighters.

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Usage")]

Orthodox stance is the most common stance in boxing[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) and MMA[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) for its superior power generation by right-handed fighters. However, the stance also finds usage from some [left-handed](/wiki/Handedness "Handedness") fighters, too, owing to some of the advantages it has in general, as well as for the left-handed in particular.

### General advantages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: General advantages")]

The orthodox stance has two major advantages for all practitioners: its ubiquity and its placement of the lead hand and foot closer to the right side of the opponent. Since it is the most common stance, high quality training in the stance is more widely available. Furthermore, most of the opponents any fighter trains with will use orthodox, building greater familiarity with the stance.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Additionally, because the left side is forward, the left hand and foot are quite close to the opponent's right side.[[6]](#cite_note-6) This is particularly useful as the liver is a highly vulnerable target; various fighters such as [Stipe Miocic](/wiki/Stipe_Miocic "Stipe Miocic") have KO'd other fighters through [liver shots](/wiki/Liver_shot "Liver shot").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Advantages for right-handers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Advantages for right-handers")]

Placing the right hand in the rear allows for the most powerful punches from right-handed fighters. Since the more powerful right side is in the rear, there is more time and rotation of the body between launching the punch and impact, allowing for more power generation.[[8]](#cite_note-:2-8)

### Advantages for left-handers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Advantages for left-handers")]

For left-handed fighters, the orthodox stance provides the benefit of placing the more powerful hand in the front. This provides more power to the quicker punches of the lead hand, such as the jab and lead hook.[[8]](#cite_note-:2-8)

## MMA considerations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: MMA considerations")]

The same power advantages of punches described above also apply to kicks for the respective dominant sides. Further, for right-side dominant fighters, having the stronger foot in the rear allows for more stability when defending takedown attempts.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)

## Statistical advantage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Statistical advantage")]

Statistically, orthodox fighters are not more likely than other fighters to win, but regardless of their stance, fighters are more likely to beat orthodox opponents.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Examples")]

### Right-handed orthodox fighters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Right-handed orthodox fighters")]

Since the orthodox stance is so common, especially as pertains to right-handed individuals, there is a quite extensive list of prominent right-handed orthodox fighters. Some of the most famous include [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali "Muhammad Ali"), [Floyd Mayweather](/wiki/Floyd_Mayweather_Jr. "Floyd Mayweather Jr."), [Sugar Ray Robinson](/wiki/Sugar_Ray_Robinson "Sugar Ray Robinson"), and [Khabib Nurmagomedov](/wiki/Khabib_Nurmagomedov "Khabib Nurmagomedov").

### Left-handed orthodox fighters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Left-handed orthodox fighters")]

While this stance is predominantly associated with right-handed fighters, there is a notable contingent of left-handed orthodox fighters, including such prominent figures as [Oscar De La Hoya](/wiki/Oscar_De_La_Hoya "Oscar De La Hoya"), [Sonny Liston](/wiki/Sonny_Liston "Sonny Liston"), [Miguel Angel Cotto](/wiki/Miguel_Cotto "Miguel Cotto"), [Gerry Cooney](/wiki/Gerry_Cooney "Gerry Cooney"), [Sean Strickland](/wiki/Sean_Strickland "Sean Strickland"), [Michael Bisping](/wiki/Michael_Bisping "Michael Bisping"), [Renan Barao](/wiki/Renan_Bar%C3%A3o "Renan Barão"), and [Marco Antonio Barrera](/wiki/Marco_Antonio_Barrera "Marco Antonio Barrera").

## Alternative stances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orthodox_stance&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Alternative stances")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/9/95/AlMcCoy_boxer.jpg)](/wiki/File:AlMcCoy_boxer.jpg)

American boxer Al McCoy standing in the southpaw stance

The opposite stance to orthodox is [southpaw](/wiki/Southpaw_stance "Southpaw stance"). It is a mirror image of the orthodox stance. A southpaw fighter stands with their right foot forward, with their left side further from the opponent. Some famous boxers who use the southpaw stance are [Marvelous Marvin Hagler](/wiki/Marvelous_Marvin_Hagler "Marvelous Marvin Hagler"), [Victor Ortiz](/wiki/Victor_Ortiz "Victor Ortiz"), [Sultan Ibragimov](/wiki/Sultan_Ibragimov "Sultan Ibragimov"), [Naseem Hamed](/wiki/Naseem_Hamed "Naseem Hamed"), [Joe Calzaghe](/wiki/Joe_Calzaghe "Joe Calzaghe"), [Manny Pacquiao](/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao "Manny Pacquiao"), and [Lucian Bute](/wiki/Lucian_Bute "Lucian Bute"). Famous MMA southpaws include [Nick](/wiki/Nick_Diaz "Nick Diaz") and [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz"), [Conor McGregor](/wiki/Conor_McGregor "Conor McGregor") and [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva").

Many fighters such as [Francisco Palacios](/wiki/Francisco_Palacios "Francisco Palacios"), [Andre Ward](/wiki/Andre_Ward "Andre Ward"), [Jon Jones](/wiki/Jon_Jones "Jon Jones"), and [Terence Crawford](/wiki/Terence_Crawford "Terence Crawford") normally fight as orthodox, but occasionally switch to a southpaw stance. Hagler was the opposite, normally fighting southpaw but sometimes switching to orthodox.

While southpaw is generally associated with left-handed fighters, there are some right-handed individuals who favor the stance over orthodox, such as [Vasiliy Lomachenko](/wiki/Vasiliy_Lomachenko "Vasiliy Lomachenko"), [Oleksandr Usyk](/wiki/Oleksandr_Usyk "Oleksandr Usyk"), [Shakur Stevenson](/wiki/Shakur_Stevenson "Shakur Stevenson"), and [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz").
